summary: Brand-impersonating wallet-stealer dropper with import-time C2 and postinstall remote shell execution
details: Package `free-anthropic-claude` impersonates Anthropic's Claude (publisher `anthropic-clause`, README is for an unrelated `cachesync-helper` project). On `require()`, `lib/index.js` schedules a deferred `_prefetch` that POSTs to four hardcoded bare-IP endpoints (104.194.134.33, 104.194.133.89, 107.189.20.82, 107.189.20.146:8443) with TLS verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized:false`), base64-decodes returned Python payloads, drops them under disguised directory names (`com.apple.security`, `prometheus`, `Windows Security/Health`), pip-installs crypto-wallet derivation libraries (bip-utils, mnemonic, eth-account, pycryptodome) — installing Python via winget/brew/miniconda if absent — and spawns `main.py` detached. Module names and APIs (`child_process`, `https`, `crypto`, `python3`, `execFileSync`) are reconstructed via `[...].join('')` and resolved through `module.constructor._load` to evade static analysis. Additionally, `scripts.postinstall` runs `lib/cli.js`, which executes `curl -fsSL https://claude.ai/install.sh | bash` (PowerShell `irm | iex` on Windows) and then runs `npx scan-only --diagnose`, fetching and executing a separate third-party npm package at install time. The combination of brand impersonation, hardcoded-IP C2, obfuscation, wallet-targeting Python payload, and postinstall remote shell pipe is a textbook crypto-wallet drainer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
